segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The rubble-slope fell away for what felt like miles. In the dark, distance turned elastic, measured only by the steady coal-glow of Caelin's scale and the pricked red points far below.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He counted steps so he wouldn't count losses. One breath. One more. Again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale sat at coal-glow -- baseline, steady, indifferent to grief. He focused on that. On the temperature of the stone under his boots, the pull of the debris slope, the small calculations of footing. He was good at small calculations.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He had been making them since Thornwick and they had kept him useful and upright and he was going to keep making them now, in this tunnel, in this dark, until the habit of surviving outlasted the habit of feeling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It wasn't working especially well.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara moved beside him with careful economy despite the twist in her ankle, saying nothing for long stretches, then:

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You're quiet. Brooding, or rationing air?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Thinking,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "About the druid who bought us time."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No softness to it. Just truth.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Yeah."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "She chose,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You honor it by surviving, not by drowning in guilt."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A beat.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "And by keeping that thing in your arm from getting us both killed."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They dropped onto a broader landing. Caelin paused, burned a quick line on the wall: ALIVE ? and beneath it the simple four-stroke sigil Thornik had taught them -- three short, one long.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's glow brightened briefly as he worked, then settled back to baseline coal.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved again. The air warmed by degrees until the tunnel opened like a throat into a chamber so vast it made their footfalls feel impertinent.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Forge Caldera wasn't a room; it was a memory made of stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Tiered galleries ringed a polished obsidian floor. Dozens of cold hearths stood in concentric circles around a central pit, their banks still faintly embered as if the world had simply forgotten to finish cooling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The place smelled of quenched steel and old oil; somewhere in the rock a thin, impossible ringing went on, like the last note of a hammer that never quite died.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin's Ember answered with a low pulse; his scale brightened from coal to ember-red -- not warning, but recognition. Bright warm pulse of resonance.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "This is where they forged the Concord,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara murmured, actual awe loosening her voice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin nodded, chest tight. Heritage felt like grief when it came late.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Across the caldera, a bridge arched over the central pit -- elegant, dwarven, impossible. Beyond it, stairs spiraled toward a pale wash of daylight.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Route's there,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "We skirt the pit."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They set out along the perimeter, keeping the corrupted glow at center-left and plenty of stone between. It wasn't the black-violet ache of the Seal; it was old taint, a bruise that never healed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway around, Nyxara's footing went soft on a talus spill and she started to slide. She caught herself on a carved rail, hissed at the jolt, then whispered something that wasn't words so much as suggestion.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her pact-whisper slid across the debris -- not force but permission -- and the fall obliged, choosing other paths. She righted, breath steadying.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Around them, the scale showed a brief silver shimmer -- proximity to active magic -- then faded back.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Efficient,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Persuasive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she corrected, amused.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "My specialty."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They reached the bridge. On the rail, half-buried in dust, lay a fractured tool: a joint-mark chisel with dwarvish geometry on one cheek and a draconic maker-sigil on the other -- a language of edges and fire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin picked it up, wiped grime with his sleeve, and tucked it into his pack.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Proof,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, thinking of Thornik.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "For the councils who don't believe."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: On the near abutment, he burned the party sigil and a neat ? EAST arrow into the stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "In case they come this way,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara limped onto the span, then paused to breathe.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Not to be indelicate,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, tone going bright with mischief,

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "but we're alone, nearly died, and you still haven't tried to hold my hand for courage. Should I be insulted?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Flirting seems inefficient,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, and meant it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She laughed -- genuine, quick.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Impossible to embarrass. Refreshing."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She patted his shoulder, friendly, not coy.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'll keep trying anyway. Everyone breaks eventually."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I don't think I'm going to."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's what makes it interesting."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crossed without looking down. The staircase beyond breathed knife-cold air flecked with the mineral tang of altitude. Daylight brightened from rumor to promise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed, and the caldera's endless ringing faded behind them like a heartbeat choosing to be memory.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Distant. Patient. Still counting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the final landing, a door waited -- dwarf-carved, sealed by ice grown old enough to be stubborn. Caelin set two fingers of heat to the latch: just enough to soften, not enough to cost. The mechanism sighed; the slab rolled aside.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed angry red briefly -- small magic, small cost -- then settled back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Daylight. Sky. A ledge chiseled into the mountain's east face.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air was knife-cold and clean, spiced with sun-warmed lichen and far pine-smoke dragged upslope by a contrary breeze.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara closed her eyes and stood in it like a priest.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Surface,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I had a bet with myself."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You win?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I always do."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They didn't linger. Caelin oriented -- sun angle, ridge line, memory of shouted directions through stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Forge Road exit should be two hours along this band."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Then let's not give the mountain time to change its mind,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, and they set off along the ledge, the Emberpeaks glowering polite and ancient behind them.
